steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Label the side pair for the named angle first: opposite, adjacent, and hypotenuse. Then pick the matching shortcut, such as SOH/CAH/TOA, complementary trig values, or a special right-triangle ratio.

steps.title: Find cos(A) and determine angle A
steps.content: In right triangle ABC with right angle at C, use the cosine ratio with AC = 19 (adjacent to angle A) and AB = 38 (hypotenuse): $$\cos(A) = \frac{19}{38} = \frac{1}{2}$$ This is the cosine of a special angle: $\cos(60^{\circ}) = \frac{1}{2}$, so $A = 60^{\circ}$.
